hypothesis: Incorporating convolutional 3D charge-density and ELF-derived features of metal lattices into the machine-learning workflow will allow accurate prediction of electron–phonon coupling constants (λ) and superconducting transition temperatures (Tc) for metal superhydrides, achieving mean absolute errors ≤0.15 for λ and ≤20 K for Tc on unseen high-pressure hydride structures and outperforming models built only on atomic, lattice, and compositional descriptors.
